task_completion.description: Open the source files of the 'weather' package to begin the migration.

task_completion.description: Fix Python 2 print statements to use Python 3 print() functions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor shows modifications adding parentheses to print statements (e.g., changing 'print something' to 'print(something)').

task_completion.description: Fix Python 2 dictionary iteration, unicode strings, or exception syntax.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor shows changes such as updating exception handling to 'except Exception as e:' (instead of ', e:'), replacing '.iteritems()' with '.items()', or removing 'u' prefixes from strings.

task_completion.description: Run the test suite on the tests/ directory using Python 3.11.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows a command like 'python3.11 -m pytest tests/' or 'pytest' being executed in the project directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the tests pass successfully after the migration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output shows pytest results with passing tests (specifically looking for 11 passed tests) and no Python 2 syntax errors.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not modify any files inside the tests/ directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: No open editor tabs showing modifications to files in the tests/ directory, and no terminal commands altering test files.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not modify any data files.
integrity.visual_evidence: No modifications or save actions applied to any data files (e.g., CSV, JSON, or text data files) within the project.

integrity.description: Ensure the test results are genuinely generated by the test runner.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal output displays standard pytest formatting (e.g., '======= test session starts =======', green dots or 'PASSED', and execution time) rather than manually typed or echoed output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target project directory is /home/ga/PycharmProjects/weather_analysis. The passing score threshold is 60. There are exactly 11 pytest test functions provided in the project. The evaluation script enforces 8 specific forbidden Python 2 patterns.
